title: galium mollugo subsp. elatum
intro: Galium mollugo subsp. elatum is a subspecies of Galium mollugo, a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as bedstraw or cleaver. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. The health benefits of Galium mollugo subsp. elatum include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes, although its usage may require further research and validation.
